Short Version (~10 words): Four Columbia students go on a 15-day hunger strike
Event: Four students go on a 15-day hunger strike, demanding that Columbia create a ethnic studies department. Three years later, Center for the Study of Ethnicity and Race at Columbia is founded.

Short Version (~10 words): Columbia goes co-ed, the final Ivy Leauge to do so
Event: Columbia goes co-ed, the final Ivy Leauge to do so.

Short Version (~10 words): Annie Meyer publishes an essay that leads to the formation of Barnard
Event: Annie Meyer publishes a 2,500 word essay in The Nation arguing that New York City culture suffers from the lack of a liberal arts college for women. As a result of ongoing advocacy from herself and her associates, Barnard College is founded one year later.

Short Version (~10 words): Columbia underclassmen steals teacups from another student’s room
Event: Columbia underclassmen Thomas Shreve, Isaac Abrahams, and Cornelius Bogert steal a set of teacups from another student’s room. As punishment, the trio is "grounded" to the College Hall of King’s College.

Short Version (~10 words): The First Pulitzer Prize is awarded.
Event: The First Pulitzer Prize is awarded.

Short Version (~10 words): Columbia College tuition increases to $2,100 annually, a 1400% increase from 1900
Event: ColumbiaCollege tuition increases to $2,100 annually, a 1400% increase from the tuition in 1900

Short Version (~10 words): Freshmen steal 25 grams of uranium-238 using Pupin's tunnels
Event: Using the tunnels under Pupin Hall, two freshmen steal 25 grams of uranium-238.

Short Version (~10 words): Men dressed in Ku Klux Klan robes set fire to a cross on South Lawn.
Event: Twenty men dressed in Ku Klux Klan robes set fire to a 7-foot wooden cross on South Lawn.

Short Version (~10 words): George Washington and John Adams attend Commencement Ceremony.
Event: U.S. President George Washington and Vice President John Adams attend the Commencement Ceremony.

Short Version (~10 words): The 116th-Street Columbia University subway station is founded
Event: The 116th-Street Columbia University subway station is founded as part of the New York City Subway line.
